In March 1993, on this day, our nation witnessed one of the darkest chapters in its history. A series of coordinated bomb blasts ripped through Mumbai, killing over 250 people and injuring hundreds more. Our nation was still reeling from that devastating act of terror. Tragedy struck again five days later. In Kolkata’s crowded Bowbazar area, a powerful bomb exploded shortly after midnight. Most residents were asleep when the blast brought down two apartment buildings, trapping dozens under the rubble. At least 50 innocent lives were lost in a single moment of terror.
